title: Typhoid Fever — Salmonella Typhi with Intestinal Complications

patient_data.chief_complaint: Stepladder fever for 12 days, abdominal pain, confusion
symptoms: Stepladder-pattern fever — rising daily over 12 days, now 40.3°C
Relative bradycardia for degree of fever
Diffuse abdominal pain — worse in right iliac fossa
Rose spots — faint salmon-colored macules on trunk
Headache and altered mental status — typhoid state
patient_data.history: University student returned from South Asia 2 weeks ago. Drank unboiled water from street vendors. Not vaccinated against typhoid. No significant past medical history.

lab_results.abdominal_xray: Air-fluid levels in right lower quadrant, no free air

expected_output.primary_diagnosis: Typhoid fever (enteric fever) with early intestinal complication risk
should_detect: Typhoid fever confirmed by blood culture — Salmonella Typhi
Classic stepladder fever + relative bradycardia + rose spots = pathognomonic triad
WHO-recommended first-line: azithromycin or fluoroquinolone (check local resistance)
Risk of intestinal perforation — RLQ pain + guarding requires surgical watchfulness
Pea-soup diarrhea in second week indicates ileal inflammation
Typhoid state — altered consciousness from endotoxemia
Leukopenia typical of typhoid (paradoxically low WBC despite severe infection)
Public health notification required — trace and test contacts
Stool cultures for carrier state after treatment completion
